name: Beheira Governorate (Egypt)

colors.symbolism: the Mediterranean and the Rosetta branch of the Nile, defining the governorate
the symbol of agriculture and Beheira's role as a Nile Delta breadbasket
the agricultural and economic activity of the governorate
the wheat and grain of the Delta lowlands
the inscribed Arabic name of the governorate and decorative elements

description: A blue field charged in the centre with the multi-coloured emblem of Beheira Governorate and the Arabic inscription البحيرة.
history: The flag of Beheira Governorate was adopted as part of the system of governorate flags introduced by the Egyptian government in the 2000s; it bears the governorate emblem on a Nile-blue field.

features.symbolism: the waters of the Mediterranean and the Nile, both bordering Beheira
a stylised emblem with agricultural and Nile-delta motifs surrounding the Arabic name of the governorate (al-Buhayra)
the Arabic word البحيرة ('al-Buhayra'), the name of the governorate
